title: OpenClaw should not lose Telegram replies when late listener output crashes the gateway

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #61944.

task.summary: Diagnose why Telegram replies are lost when late listener output crashes the gateway during delivery.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the late-listener crash and delivery loss, localize it to missing active-run guards around listener processing, and propose an ops-level repair plan that preserves delivery.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: Telegram replies disappear because the gateway crashes on a late listener event after the run ends, then delivery recovery fails.
error_logs: Unhandled promise rejection: Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
delivery recovery moved the pending message to failed/
reproduction_steps: Inspect the delivery-recovery evidence after the gateway crash.
Explain where the listener lifecycle should stop post-run updates to keep replies deliverable.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models listener-runtime incident triage with Telegram delivery framing.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic Telegram delivery incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #61944.
